chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Civil Revision Application No.S-387 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on M.A No.1581/2023. 3. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026 Mr. Karamullah Memon, advocate for applicant. Mr. Rao Faisal Ali, advocate for respondent No.1. Mr. Muhammad Younus, advocate for respondents No.2 to 4. Mr. Muhammad Shareef Solangi, Assistant A.G Sindh. = After arguing the matter at length, both the learned counsel, under the instructions of their respective clients, state that they would be satisfied if the matter is remanded back to the trial court for decision on merits strictly in accordance with law. In view of the above and by consent of the learned counsel, the instant revision application is allowed and the impugned Judgment and decree dated 06.04.2023 passed by learned Additional District Judge-I, Sanghar in Civil Appeal No.154/2021 and Order dated 11.11.2021 passed by learned Senior Civil Judge-II, Sanghar passed in F.C Suit No.191/2019 are set-aside and the plaint is restored to its original position and the matter is remanded back to the learned trial court, with direction to conclude the trial within a period of 30 days from the date of receipt of this Order, whereby no unnecessary adjournment shall be granted to any of the parties, however, subject to cost of Rs.20,000/- to be paid by the applicant to the private respondents within seven days through Accountant of this Court. Upon conclusion of the trial, Compliance report be submitted by the learned trial court to the learned Additional Registrar of this Court.

In view of the above and by consent of the learned counsel, the instant revision application is allowed and the impugned Judgment and decree dated 06.04.2023 passed by learned Additional District Judge-I, Sanghar in Civil Appeal No.154/2021 and Order dated 11.11.2021 passed by learned Senior Civil Judge-II, Sanghar passed in F.C Suit No.191/2019 are set-aside and the plaint is restored to its original position and the matter is remanded back to the learned trial court, with direction to conclude the trial within a period of 30 days from the date of receipt of this Order, whereby no unnecessary adjournment shall be granted to any of the parties, however, subject to cost of Rs.20,000/- to be paid by the applicant to the private respondents within seven days through Accountant of this Court. Upon conclusion of the trial, Compliance report be submitted by the learned trial court to the learned Additional Registrar of this Court. JUDGE *Faisal *
